next_steps: Liver biopsy with histopathology, immunohistochemistry for melanoma, and special stains for infection
Serum ferritin, soluble CD25, and triglycerides to screen for HLH
Anaplasmosis PCR and peripheral blood smear for morulae
Initiate corticosteroids if drug-induced liver injury or autoimmune hepatitis is confirmed on biopsy
Monitor liver function tests and hematologic parameters daily

differential_diagnoses: Drug-induced liver injury (DILI) with cholestatic pattern
Occult cholangitis (due to stone or tumor)
next_steps: Perform MRCP to evaluate for occult biliary obstruction
Obtain serum ferritin, triglycerides, fibrinogen, and soluble CD25 (IL-2 receptor) to evaluate for HLH
Perform peripheral blood smear review for morulae (anaplasmosis) and evidence of hemophagocytosis
Perform liver biopsy for histopathology, immunohistochemistry for melanoma, and special stains
Check morning serum cortisol and ACTH to assess for adrenal insufficiency
Maintain patient on supportive care and monitor liver function tests and hematologic parameters daily
